original_hypothesis.title: Registry onlyVaultMaintainer Modifier Uses OR Instead of AND, Requiring Both Maintainer AND Emergency Roles
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `onlyVaultMaintainer` modifier in Registry.sol uses `||` in its revert condition: `if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`. By De Morgan's law, this passes ONLY when the caller is BOTH the vault maintainer AND holds the global EMERGENCY_ROLE. All other vault modifiers (onlyVaultGoverner, onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock) correctly use `&&`, passing when EITHER condition is met. This locks `addConnector`, `updateConnectorTrustedTokens`, and `removeTrustedPosition` unless the maintainer address also holds EMERGENCY_ROLE.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Comparison with other modifiers in the same contract clearly shows the inconsistency. The `onlyVaultGoverner` uses `&&` in its revert condition, which is the standard OR-either-role pattern. The onlyVaultMaintainer uses `||`, which is mathematically different.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deployment script updateRegistry.ts shows separate addresses used but also grants EMERGENCY_ROLE to the same address, possibly as a workaround for this bug

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false) — uses || where && is intended, compare with onlyVaultGoverner at lines 38-41 which uses &&

preconditions: Vault maintainer address does NOT hold the global EMERGENCY_ROLE
Caller attempts to use addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, or removeTrustedPosition

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Boolean OR (||) in revert condition instead of AND (&&)
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: onlyVaultMaintainer requires BOTH roles instead of EITHER, unlike all other vault modifiers
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No alternative path for vault maintainer without emergency role

analysis_questions: Is the vault maintainer expected to hold EMERGENCY_ROLE in production?
Are addConnector and updateConnectorTrustedTokens reachable in production?
Does the deployment script work around this by granting EMERGENCY_ROLE to the maintainer?
validation_requirements: Confirm by testing that a maintainer-only address (without EMERGENCY_ROLE) cannot call addConnector
Compare with onlyVaultGoverner to confirm the intended pattern

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: In a production deployment where vault maintainer and emergency are separate addresses (as intended by the governance design), the maintainer cannot add connectors, update trusted tokens, or remove positions. This blocks vault administration and prevents the vault from adapting to changing DeFi conditions.
prerequisites: Vault maintainer address differs from EMERGENCY_ROLE holder

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Vault becomes unable to update connectors or trusted tokens, potentially trapping funds in deprecated protocols
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires granting EMERGENCY_ROLE to maintainer as workaround, or code fix

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for maintainer-only access to addConnector without emergency role

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Easily detectable by attempting to call addConnector as vault maintainer without emergency role

validation_result.mechanism: boolean operator error in access control modifier
validation_result.impact_summary: The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier requires callers to be BOTH the vault maintainer AND hold EMERGENCY_ROLE, instead of EITHER role. This locks addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, and removeTrustedPosition unless the maintainer also holds the global EMERGENCY_ROLE.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The onlyVaultMaintainer modifier at Registry.sol lines 26-30 uses the `||` (OR) operator in its revert condition:

`if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`

By De Morgan's law, this reverts unless BOTH conditions are false, meaning the caller must be the maintainer AND have EMERGENCY_ROLE simultaneously.

In contrast, the onlyVaultGoverner (lines 38-41) and onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock (lines 32-36) modifiers correctly use `&&` (AND) in their revert conditions, which allows EITHER the specified role OR EMERGENCY_ROLE holders to pass.

This is an unambiguous code defect — the operator should be `&&` not `||`.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment:
Direct comparison of the three modifiers in Registry.sol proves the inconsistency:

1. onlyVaultMaintainer (line 27): uses `||` — requires BOTH roles
2. onlyVaultGoverner (line 39): uses `&&` — requires EITHER role
3. onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock (line 33): uses `&&` — requires EITHER role

The deployment script (updateRegistry.ts) uses the same address `0x8E95f959f1Bd3C4A3Be2bda6089155012fF1a37b` for all vault roles AND grants EMERGENCY_ROLE to it, which masks the bug in that specific deployment.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Uses `||` instead of `&&` in the revert condition. By De Morgan's law, this passes only when (msg.sender == maintainer) AND (hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == true). The intended behavior (matching onlyVaultGoverner and onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock) is to pass when EITHER condition is met, which requires `&&` in the revert condition.
This is the CORRECT pattern using `&&`. It reverts only when msg.sender is NOT the governor AND does NOT have EMERGENCY_ROLE, meaning it passes if EITHER condition is met.
Another instance of the correct pattern using `&&`, further confirming the `||` in onlyVaultMaintainer is a bug.

calling_sequence: 3. Modifier checks: msg.sender != vaults[vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false
4. If caller IS the maintainer but does NOT have EMERGENCY_ROLE, the first condition is false but second is true, so || evaluates to true → REVERT
5. If caller has EMERGENCY_ROLE but is NOT the maintainer, the first condition is true → REVERT
6. Only passes if caller is BOTH maintainer AND has EMERGENCY_ROLE
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: Registry.sol line 27: The revert condition `msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer || hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false` uses || (logical OR). For the revert NOT to trigger, both sub-conditions must be false: (1) msg.sender must equal maintainer, AND (2) hasRole must return true. This differs from the intended behavior seen in onlyVaultGoverner (line 39) and onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock (line 33), which use && and allow either condition to pass independently.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Vault maintainer address differs from EMERGENCY_ROLE holder
config_validation.deployment_evidence: updateRegistry.ts shows all vault addresses set to the same address 0x8E95f959f1Bd3C4A3Be2bda6089155012fF1a37b, and updateRole() grants EMERGENCY_ROLE to the same address. This masks the bug in this specific deployment but does not fix the code defect.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: In any deployment where the vault maintainer and EMERGENCY_ROLE are held by different addresses (which is the intended governance separation), the maintainer cannot call addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, or removeTrustedPosition. This blocks essential vault administration: adding new strategy connectors, updating trusted tokens for connectors, and removing stale trusted positions. The vault becomes locked in its initial configuration, unable to adapt to changing DeFi conditions or respond to protocol deprecations.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL severity. Three essential vault administration functions (addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, removeTrustedPosition) are inaccessible to the vault maintainer unless they also hold the global EMERGENCY_ROLE. In production deployments with proper role separation, this renders vault maintenance impossible without workarounds. The deployment script's use of the same address for all roles masks the bug but represents a centralization risk rather than a fix.
recommended_mitigations: Change `||` to `&&` in the onlyVaultMaintainer modifier at Registry.sol line 27: `if (msg.sender != vaults[_vaultId].maintainer && hasRole(EMERGENCY_ROLE, msg.sender) == false)`
Add unit tests verifying that a maintainer-only address (without EMERGENCY_ROLE) can call addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, and removeTrustedPosition
Add unit tests verifying that an EMERGENCY_ROLE holder (who is not the maintainer) can also call these functions
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Registry with separate addresses for governer, maintainer, and emergency. 2. Add a vault with distinct maintainer and emergency addresses. 3. As the maintainer (without EMERGENCY_ROLE), attempt to call addConnector — expect revert with UnauthorizedAccess(). 4. As the EMERGENCY_ROLE holder (without being maintainer), attempt the same — expect revert. 5. Grant EMERGENCY_ROLE to the maintainer, then call addConnector — expect success, confirming the AND requirement.
uncertainties: Whether production deployments actually use separate addresses for maintainer and EMERGENCY_ROLE (the test deployment uses the same address for all roles)
additional_testing_needed: Fuzz test all three modifiers with various role combinations to verify consistent behavior
Verify no other functions rely on the current (buggy) behavior of onlyVaultMaintainer
